title: Punk Rocker Baby Birds Win Big Photo Prize!
summary: Have you ever seen a baby bird with a mohawk? Well, now you can! The grand prize winner of the Smithsonian Magazine's 23rd annual photo contest is a picture of three egret chicks that look like tiny punk rockers. Their feathers stick up in spiky points, just like a punk hairstyle. The photographer, Jacqueline Burke, is a 64-year-old veterinarian from Pennsylvania. She took the photo at a rookery (a place where birds breed) in New Jersey. "They were squabbling nonstop," she says. The chicks were no bigger than golf balls when curled up! Their huge parent stands in the background, with long legs and big feet. Over 17,000 photos from 108 countries were entered. Other winners include a barefoot woman at a Hindu festival, a 14-year-old Navajo girl on her horse, and a colorful chameleon. One photo shows a giant ring-shaped building in China. Another shows a Mundari child cleaning a bull's horns with mud. Burke started taking photos in 2014. She says, "We need to save species like this and save moments like this." Cool, right?
